ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002490 covers hot dog as a food product category; no specific chicken hot dog variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Hot dog / frankfurter type sausage in bun
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers frankfurter/hot dog type products; no chicken-specific variant code available. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Jolly Chicken Hot Dog serving consists of a steamed soft bun with one chicken frankfurter sausage and condiments, weighing approximately 120 g.

nutrient_source.title: Frankfurter, chicken — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 07025); Hot dog bun — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18030); composite estimate for assembled product
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct Jollibee Singapore nutrition panel available in approved sources. Nutrient values estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for chicken frankfurter (NDB 07025, ~60 g) and plain hot dog bun (NDB 18030, ~43 g) with minor condiment contribution, then normalised to per-100 g. This is a medium-confidence estimate. Values should be updated if an official Jollibee Singapore nutrition disclosure becomes available in an approved source.

gi_source.title: White bread and mixed wheat-flour bun analogous GI estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chicken hot dog in a soft bun. GI estimated at 52 based on the GI of a soft white wheat hot dog bun (GI ~50–55, analogous to white bread GI 50 per University of Sydney GI Database) and the negligible glycemic contribution of the chicken sausage component. Glycemic load per 120 g serving calculated as (52 × 26.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.2) / 100 = 16.5 g. Estimate is medium confidence.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~744 mg per 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this product due to its high sodium content of approximately 620 mg per 100 g (≈744 mg per serving), which represents about 31–37% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load and refined carbohydrate from white bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of this product's moderate glycemic load (~16.5 per serving) driven by the refined wheat bun; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods or non-starchy vegetables is advisable to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, sodium, and ultra-processed meat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit intake of this ultra-processed chicken sausage product due to its combined saturated fat (~3.1 g/100 g), elevated sodium (~620 mg/100 g), and small trans fat content, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jolly Chicken Hot Dog is a fast food item served at Jollibee restaurants, including the Bugis Village outlet in Singapore. It consists of a soft, lightly steamed wheat-flour hot dog bun encasing a halal-certified chicken frankfurter sausage, typically dressed with mayonnaise, mustard, and ketchup. The sausage is mildly seasoned, with a smooth, slightly springy texture, and the bun is pillowy and slightly sweet. The overall flavour profile is savoury with mild tang from the condiments. As an ultra-processed meat product, it is energy-dense at approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (~10.8 g/100 g), moderate fat (~11.2 g/100 g), and a significant carbohydrate contribution (~26.4 g/100 g) from the refined wheat bun. Sodium content is notably high at ~620 mg/100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at ~16.5, relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The product is halal-certified and dairy-free but contains gluten and soy allergens. It is a popular affordable fast food option in Singapore, priced at approximately SGD 5.00, and is commonly consumed as a snack or light meal.

Is the Jolly Chicken Hot Dog halal?

Jollibee hot dog GI glycemic index

Jolly Chicken Hot Dog allergens gluten

confidence_reason: No official Jollibee Singapore nutrition disclosure was available in approved sources at time of processing. Nutrient values were estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for chicken frankfurter (NDB 07025) and plain hot dog bun (NDB 18030), normalised to per-100 g. GI was estimated by analogy to soft white wheat bun from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA SR Legacy components are well-characterised approved sources and the composite methodology is standard practice for assembled fast food items.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a composite estimate from USDA SR Legacy NDB 07025 (chicken frankfurter) and NDB 18030 (hot dog bun); no direct Jollibee Singapore nutrition panel was available in approved sources. Values should be updated if an official disclosure becomes available. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite product in the source database. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this composite. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this composite. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this composite. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — chicken frankfurter and wheat bun contain negligible vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central; confirmed zero per source. 7. GI set to 52 by analogous estimate from soft white wheat bun GI data (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this product exists. 8. The '(D)' suffix in the input food name 'Jolly Chicken Hot Dog (D)' likely denotes a delivery-only menu variant or a size/combo designation on field survey; it has been dropped from the canonical food_name as it is not part of the standard product name. 9. Halal status confirmed via Jollibee Singapore cuisine tags in source data.

improvement_actions_taken: Dropped '(D)' delivery suffix from canonical food_name; documented in notes.
Composed nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy NDB 07025 and NDB 18030 as no direct approved-source panel was available.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central.
Estimated GI at 52 by analogy to soft white wheat bun from University of Sydney GI Database; documented estimation basis in gi_source and notes.
Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent — all language-specific strings appear verbatim in flat aliases.
Set ontology match types to 'closest_parent' or 'none' only; no invented codes used.
Included zero-macro justification for vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 using 'confirmed zero' phrase in notes.
